archetype: Real-Time Multi-Spectral LIDAR Point Cloud Registration & Structural Integrity Anomaly Detector for High-Voltage Overhead Transmission Line Corrugated Insulator Flashover Prediction
domain: High-Voltage Power Grid Asset Management & Predictive Maintenance
description: A specialized edge-compute system that fuses data from synchronized terrestrial LiDAR and hyperspectral imaging sensors mounted on drone swarms to detect micro-fractures and conductive contamination on ceramic insulators before flashover occurs. It is used by grid reliability engineers to prevent catastrophic brownouts, requiring sub-millisecond fusion of Terabyte-scale point clouds with real-time atmospheric refractivity models to distinguish between harmless condensation and dangerous conductive salt deposits.
rules: MUST ingest and process raw .las/.laz LiDAR point clouds and hyperspectral radiance matrices in real-time (sub-50ms latency) using IEEE 1588 PTP for sensor synchronization.
MUST adhere to IEC 60815 standards for污秽 (pollution) classification, calculating equivalent salt deposit density (ESDD) based on spectral reflectance ratios.
MUST implement deterministic scheduling for edge inference to guarantee worst-case execution time (WCET) compliance in high-interference RF environments near substations.
MUST utilize non-volatile memory architectures to log pre-fault waveform data for forensic analysis in compliance with NERC CIP-007 cyber-physical security requirements.
MUST dynamically compensate for drone vibration and atmospheric scattering effects using Kalman Filtering on the point cloud registration pipeline.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use standard HTTP/REST APIs for sensor data ingestion; the latency and overhead will cause the system to miss transient flashover precursors.
DO NOT rely on cloud-only processing; network latency to central data centers makes real-time hazard avoidance impossible for autonomous drone swarms.
DO NOT use generic object detection models trained on RGB images alone; they fail to detect invisible conductive contaminants on non-metallic insulator surfaces.
key_technical_challenges: Aligning multi-sensor data streams (LiDAR, thermal, hyperspectral) with millimeter-level precision despite high-frequency drone oscillation and wind shear.
Performing real-time 3D Voronoi tessellation and volumetric analysis on massive point clouds directly on embedded edge hardware with limited thermal headroom.
Developing algorithms to differentiate between harmless fog/mist and electrically conductive salt deposition using only spectral signature analysis in low-light conditions.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Provides memory safety guarantees with zero-cost abstractions for deterministic real-time signal processing.
recommended_stack.framework: ROS 2 (Humble) with Real-Time Kernel (PREEMPT_RT) — Native support for hard real-time communication between sensor drivers and inference engines.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB with PostGIS extension — Optimized for high-velocity time-series sensor data and geospatial indexing of grid assets.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis with Resilient Persistence — For rapid state synchronization between drone swarm nodes.
recommended_stack.message_broker: NATS — High-performance, low-latency messaging suitable for edge-to-edge drone swarm coordination.
special_services: Spectral Unmixing Engine (Python/C++ hybrid) for isolating contaminant signatures
Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) for predicting flashover probability based on humidity and voltage load

compliance_flags: IEC 61850 (Communications networks and systems for substations)
